notes: Real news view counts are usually not public; this version uses open RSS sources and avoids paywalled links.
Add comma-separated RSS URLs to NEWS_FEED_URLS in .env.local to include TLDR-style newsletters or other readable feeds.
AI items must pass freshness and evidence-chain gates; application items need at least two anchors with one primary anchor.
First-read summaries and AI application risk analysis use DeepSeek only, not GPT; traceable source extracts are used when DeepSeek is unavailable.

articles.title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
articles.selectionReason: Open, non-paywalled RSS candidate; recent item across configured news feeds
articles.summary: 一名在刚果民主共和国感染埃博拉病毒的美国援助工作者已抵达德国柏林接受治疗，而特朗普政府同时禁止美国公民从该国乘坐商业航班返回美国。该事件凸显了国际公共卫生应对机制与政治决策之间的紧张关系，值得关注全球疫情下的跨境医疗与旅行限制。
articles.failureAnalysis: 关键启示：国际疫情应对中，医疗救治与政治管控可能产生冲突，需平衡公共卫生安全与个人权利。
articles.sourceExcerpt: Aid worker flown to Berlin as Trump administration bars Americans from traveling to US on commercial flights A US national who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another American infected with Ebola in the DRC was treated in Berlin. Meanwhile, the Trump administration on Monday said it was blocking American citizens in ⁠the DRC from traveling to the US on commercial flights, Reuters reported, citing a White House official.

en.summary: An American aid worker infected with Ebola in the DRC has arrived in Berlin for treatment, while the Trump administration bars US citizens from traveling to the US on commercial flights from the region. This highlights the tension between international health response and political travel restrictions, making it relevant for understanding global pandemic management.
en.failureAnalysis: Key takeaway: In global health crises, medical treatment and political controls may conflict, requiring a balance between public health safety and individual rights.
en.title: US Ebola Patient Arrives in Germany for Treatment Amid Travel Ban

articles.title: U.S. to reinstate Hormuz blockade. And, states sue over Paramount-Warner deal
articles.selectionReason: Open, non-paywalled RSS candidate; recent item across configured news feeds
articles.summary: 美国计划今日恢复对霍尔木兹海峡的封锁，同时多个州提起诉讼以阻止派拉蒙与华纳兄弟的合并。这篇文章值得阅读，因为它涉及地缘政治紧张局势和媒体行业重大交易，对理解国际动态和商业格局有参考价值。
articles.failureAnalysis: 关键要点：美国恢复霍尔木兹海峡封锁可能加剧中东紧张局势，而派拉蒙-华纳合并诉讼则凸显反垄断监管对媒体整合的制约。
articles.sourceExcerpt: The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros.

en.summary: The U.S. plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today, while several states sue to block the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger. This article is worth reading for its coverage of geopolitical tensions and a major media industry deal, offering insights into international dynamics and business regulation.
en.failureAnalysis: Key takeaway: The reinstated Hormuz blockade could escalate Middle East tensions, while the lawsuit over the Paramount-Warner merger highlights antitrust scrutiny on media consolidation.
en.title: U.S. to Reinstate Hormuz Blockade; States Sue Over Paramount-Warner Merger

articles.failureAnalysis: 关键要点：美国重启霍尔木兹海峡封锁，伊朗强硬回应，凸显中东地缘政治风险加剧。

articles.title: DietrichGebert/ponytail
articles.selectionReason: AI source passed freshness and evidence-chain gate: GitHub + Community signal
articles.summary: 该项目提出一种让AI代理模仿“最懒资深开发者”思维的方法，核心是减少不必要的代码编写。它通过GitHub上8.2万星标获得关注，暗示开发者对“少写代码、多思考”理念的强烈共鸣。值得阅读是因为它挑战了AI辅助编程中“生成越多越好”的默认假设，可能改变AI代码生成工具的设计方向。
articles.failureAnalysis: 核心风险在于“懒惰”与“正确性”的平衡难以量化——资深开发者的“懒”基于深厚经验，而AI可能错误简化关键逻辑。项目缺乏具体实现细节和验证案例，仅靠理念难以说服企业采用。部署路径不明确，用户价值依赖对“代码质量”的主观判断，而非可测量的效率指标。工程上，让AI自主决定“不写代码”可能引入隐蔽的缺陷，增加调试成本。
articles.sourceExcerpt: GitHub repository with 82882 stars and recent public activity. Description: Makes your AI agent think like the laziest senior dev in the room. The best code is the code you never wrote.

en.summary: This project proposes a method to make AI agents emulate the 'laziest senior developer' mindset, focusing on reducing unnecessary code. Its 82k GitHub stars indicate strong resonance with the 'write less, think more' philosophy. Worth reading as it challenges the default assumption that more code generation is always better, potentially reshaping AI coding tool design.
en.failureAnalysis: The key risk is the unquantifiable balance between 'laziness' and correctness—senior devs' laziness stems from deep experience, while AI may oversimplify critical logic. Without concrete implementation or validation, the idea struggles to convince enterprises for adoption.
en.title: Ponytail: Making AI Think Like the Laziest Senior Dev

articles.title: [AINews] Codex usage up >10x in 6 months to 7M users, +1M in the past ~day; did Codex overtake Claude Code??

articles.summary: 文章指出，OpenAI的Codex在6个月内用户量增长超过10倍，达到700万，最近一天内新增100万，可能已超越Claude Code。这反映了AI编程工具市场的快速扩张和竞争格局变化，值得关注其背后的技术趋势和用户偏好。
articles.failureAnalysis: 关键启示：AI编程助手用户增长迅猛，但需注意不同工具间的功能差异和实际使用场景，避免盲目跟风。
articles.sourceExcerpt: a quiet day lets us fact check some numbers against the sound of silence of Claude Code reporting...

en.summary: The article reports that OpenAI's Codex has grown over 10x in users in 6 months, reaching 7 million, with 1 million added in the past day, potentially surpassing Claude Code. This highlights rapid expansion and competition in AI coding tools, making it worth reading for understanding market dynamics.
en.failureAnalysis: Key takeaway: AI coding assistants are growing fast, but consider functional differences and real-world usage before making decisions.
en.title: Codex Usage Surges 10x in 6 Months to 7M Users, Overtaking Claude Code?

articles.title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning
articles.selectionReason: Open full-text paper; topic ai; quality signal passed; application scenario is clear.

paperCard.problem: 现有AI模型要么把结构变成文本（丢失物理信息），要么只输出预测结果而不解释原因。科学家需要既能准确预测又能展示推理过程的工具。
paperCard.method: SciReasoner将蛋白质、分子和晶体的三维结构离散化为一种“结构感知词汇”，然后把这些词汇和语言指令一起输入大语言模型，让模型在生成答案时能引用具体的结构证据。
paperCard.difference: 以前的方法要么用文本描述结构（导致推理依赖语言关联），要么用专门的预测模型（不展示中间证据）。SciReasoner把结构本身当作可引用的证据单元，在推理过程中明确展示。
paperCard.innovation: 创新在于“原生结构推理”：结构词汇不是辅助描述，而是推理轨迹中可寻址、可检查的证据单元，实现了结构-推理的深度融合。
paperCard.implementation: 首先用领域专用编码器（如ConfSeq、Foldseek、SLICES）将结构转为保留物理信息的文本表示；然后通过多阶段训练（预热、全参数、退火）对齐结构词汇和语言；最后用强化学习训练模型在推理中引用结构证据。
paperCard.applications: 蛋白质功能预测（尤其是低同源蛋白）、化学反应路线设计（逆合成分析）、晶体材料性质预测（如带隙）、科学问答等。
technicalTerms: 原生结构推理：模型在推理时直接引用结构中的原子、键或对称性等具体证据，而不是仅靠文本联想。
结构感知词汇：将三维结构拆解成保留物理意义的“单词”，比如把分子中的苯环作为一个整体token，避免被拆散。
自举原生结构推理：用强化学习让模型自己学会在推理中正确使用结构证据，而不需要人工标注推理过程。

articles.summary: 这篇论文提出了一个名为SciReasoner的多模态科学基础模型，能够对蛋白质、小分子和晶体等结构进行“原生结构推理”，在预测性质的同时生成可检查的推理过程。它值得阅读，因为它在86个基准测试中的67个上达到了最先进性能，并且专家评估认为其推理质量优于或媲美前沿大语言模型。
articles.failureAnalysis: 论文未提及模型在极低数据或全新结构域上的泛化能力，且其依赖的离散化结构词汇可能丢失部分连续几何细节。
articles.sourceExcerpt: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning Chen Tang1,2鈥? Shixiang Tang 2,1, Pengze Li 1,4, Encheng Su 1,8, Jun Yao 1,8, Jiabei Xiao 1,2, Yuqi Shi 9,10, Jielan Li 1, Hongxia Hao 1, Zhangyang Gao 1, Fang Wu 11, Ben Fei 1,2, Xiangyu Yue 2, Pan Tan 1, Bozitao Zhong3, Jinouwen Zhang 1, Aoran Wang 1, Yan Lu 2,1, Jiaheng Liu 6,1, Xinzhu Ma 1, Liang Hong 3, Mingyue Zheng 9,10, Phil Torr 7, Bowen Zhou 1, Wanli Ouyang 1,2, Lei Bai 1 1Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, China. 2The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Hong Kong.

en.summary: This paper introduces SciReasoner, a multimodal scientific foundation model that performs 'native structural reasoning' on proteins, small molecules, and crystals, generating inspectable reasoning traces alongside predictions. It is worth reading because it achieves state-of-the-art performance on 67 out of 86 benchmarks, and expert evaluation rates its reasoning quality as preferred or comparable to frontier LLMs.
en.failureAnalysis: The paper does not discuss generalization to extremely low-data or novel structural domains, and its discretized structural vocabulary may lose some continuous geometric details.
en.title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning

paperCard.problem: Existing AI models either convert structures into text (losing physical information) or output predictions without explanation. Scientists need tools that are both accurate and can show their reasoning.
paperCard.method: SciReasoner discretizes 3D structures of proteins, molecules, and crystals into a 'structure-aware vocabulary', then feeds these tokens with language instructions into a large language model, allowing it to cite specific structural evidence when generating answers.
paperCard.difference: Previous methods either describe structures in text (making reasoning rely on linguistic associations) or use specialized prediction models (without showing intermediate evidence). SciReasoner treats structures as citable evidence units within the reasoning trajectory.
paperCard.innovation: The innovation is 'native structural reasoning': structural tokens are not auxiliary descriptors but addressable evidence units in the reasoning trajectory, enabling deep integration of structure and reasoning.
paperCard.implementation: First, domain-specific encoders (e.g., ConfSeq, Foldseek, SLICES) convert structures into text representations preserving physical meaning; then, multi-stage training (warm-up, full-parameter, annealing) aligns structural tokens with language; finally, reinforcement learning trains the model to cite structural evidence during reasoning.
paperCard.applications: Protein function prediction (especially for low-homology proteins), retrosynthesis (chemical reaction planning), crystal property prediction (e.g., band gap), scientific question answering.
technicalTerms: native structural reasoning: the model directly cites specific structural evidence (atoms, bonds, symmetry) during reasoning, not just text associations.
structure-aware vocabulary: 3D structures are broken into physically meaningful 'words', e.g., treating a benzene ring as a single token to avoid fragmentation.
self-bootstrapped native structural reasoning: reinforcement learning teaches the model to correctly use structural evidence in reasoning without requiring human-annotated reasoning traces.

articles.title: Graph-Regularized Deep Learning for EEG-Based Emotion Recognition with Psychologically-Grounded Label Structure
articles.selectionReason: Open full-text paper; topic brain_computer_interface; quality signal passed; application scenario is clear.

paperCard.problem: 传统脑电情绪识别方法把不同情绪当成独立、等距离的类别，比如把“开心”错分成“悲伤”和错分成“害怕”的惩罚一样。但心理学上，情绪之间有亲疏关系（比如“悲伤”和“害怕”都是负面情绪，更相似），所以传统方法不合理，导致模型学到的特征不够好。
paperCard.method: 作者构建了一个“情绪图”，把每种情绪看作图上的一个节点，用边连接心理上相近的情绪（比如“悲伤”和“害怕”相连，“开心”只连“中性”）。然后，在训练深度学习模型时，加入三种正则化惩罚，让模型更倾向于把情绪错分到图上相邻的类别，而不是跨越大距离。
paperCard.difference: 之前的方法要么用标准交叉熵损失（平等对待所有错误），要么用简单的标签平滑（均匀分配概率）。这篇论文首次系统地将情绪的心理拓扑结构（基于Russell的环形模型）融入训练目标，并且提供了三种不同复杂度的正则化方法，适用于不同场景。
paperCard.innovation: 创新点在于：1）把情绪的心理结构（环形模型）转化为图，并定义了三种基于图的正则化损失（图标签平滑、图拉普拉斯损失、切片Wasserstein距离），从不同数学角度惩罚不合理错误；2）提出“邻近违规率”指标，专门衡量模型是否做出心理上合理的错误预测；3）验证了该方法对多种深度学习架构（Transformer、CNN-Transformer混合、图神经网络）都有效。
paperCard.implementation: 具体实现分三步：1）根据Russell的环形模型，在二维“愉悦度-唤醒度”空间上定位每种情绪，连接距离近的情绪形成图；2）在训练时，除了标准分类损失，还加上三种正则化损失之一：图标签平滑（简单，把概率分配给相邻情绪）、图拉普拉斯损失（中等，用图拉普拉斯矩阵的伪逆计算全局距离）、切片Wasserstein距离（复杂，用最优传输衡量分布差异）；3）通过固定权重或自适应权重（自动学习每个任务的权重）平衡分类和正则化。
paperCard.applications: 1）心理健康监测：更准确地识别患者的情绪状态，辅助诊断和治疗；2）情感脑机接口：让设备更自然地理解用户情绪，比如调整音乐或游戏体验；3）消费级脑电设备（如Muse、Emotiv）：提升日常情绪追踪的可靠性；4）任何需要理解情绪关系的场景，如人机交互、教育、娱乐。
technicalTerms: 图正则化：在训练模型时，加入一个基于图结构的惩罚项，让模型学会尊重情绪之间的亲疏关系。
环形模型：一种心理学理论，把情绪放在一个二维圆环上，横轴是愉悦度（正面/负面），纵轴是唤醒度（兴奋/平静）。
图拉普拉斯矩阵：一种数学工具，用来描述图上节点之间的连接关系，可以计算节点间的“通勤距离”。
切片Wasserstein距离：一种衡量两个概率分布差异的方法，通过随机投影降低计算复杂度，这里用来衡量预测分布和真实分布之间的“运输成本”。
邻近违规率：一个评价指标，计算模型错误预测中，那些跨越大距离（心理上不合理）的错误所占的比例。

articles.summary: 这篇论文提出了一种图正则化深度学习框架，用于基于脑电信号的情绪识别。它通过将情绪之间的心理相似性建模成图结构，并引入三种正则化策略，显著提升了情绪分类的准确性和心理合理性。值得一读是因为它解决了传统方法忽略情绪内在关联的问题，且方法通用，适用于多种深度学习模型。
articles.failureAnalysis: 主要局限在于实验仅在两个公开数据集（SEED-IV和SEED-V）上进行，且情绪类别较少（4-5类），在更多类别或真实临床场景下的效果有待验证。此外，计算复杂度随情绪类别增加而上升，可能影响大规模应用。
articles.sourceExcerpt: Graph-Regularized Deep Learning for EEG-Based Emotion Recognition with Psychologically-Grounded Label Structure Dongyang Kuang, Zizheng Ma, Yushan Zhang, and Xiaocong Zeng School of Mathematics (Zhuhai), Sun Yat-sen University, Guangzhou, China Abstract.EEG-based emotion recognition is critical for mental health monitoring and affective brain-computer interfaces, yet existing deep learning approaches often treat emotion classes as isolated labels, ignor- ingtheirpsychologicalinterdependencies.Weproposeagraph-regularized learning framework that conceptualizes emotions as nodes in a graph where edges encode proximity based on dimensional emotion theories. We adapt three complementary regularization strategies鈥擥raph Label Smoothing (intuitive soft labeling), Commuting distance on graph via Graph Laplacian (spectral graph theory), and Sliced Wasserstein Dis- tance (optimal transport on graph)鈥攐rdered by increasing computa- tional complexity. These strategies penalize model predictions that devi- ate from the established emotion topology.

en.summary: This paper proposes a graph-regularized deep learning framework for EEG-based emotion recognition. It models psychological relationships between emotions as a graph and introduces three regularization strategies to penalize psychologically implausible misclassifications, improving accuracy and clinical relevance. It is worth reading because it addresses a key limitation of standard methods and is architecture-agnostic.
en.failureAnalysis: The main limitation is that experiments are conducted on only two datasets with few emotion classes (4-5), and the effectiveness in real clinical settings with more categories remains to be validated. Computational complexity also increases with the number of emotion classes.
en.title: Graph-Regularized Deep Learning for EEG-Based Emotion Recognition with Psychologically-Grounded Label Structure

paperCard.problem: Traditional EEG emotion recognition treats all emotion classes as independent and equally distant, ignoring psychological relationships (e.g., confusing 'sad' with 'fear' is more plausible than confusing 'happy' with 'sad'). This leads to suboptimal models.
paperCard.method: The authors construct an 'emotion graph' where nodes are emotions and edges connect psychologically similar ones (based on Russell's circumplex model). They then add three graph-based regularization losses during training to penalize misclassifications that cross distant nodes in the graph.
paperCard.difference: Previous methods either use standard cross-entropy loss (treating all errors equally) or simple label smoothing. This paper is the first to systematically integrate the psychological topology of emotions into the training objective, offering three regularization strategies with different complexity.
paperCard.innovation: Innovations include: 1) Converting the psychological circumplex model into a graph and defining three graph-based losses (Graph Label Smoothing, Graph Laplacian, Sliced Wasserstein) from different mathematical perspectives; 2) Proposing a 'Proximity Violation' metric to measure psychologically plausible errors; 3) Demonstrating architecture-agnostic benefits across transformers, CNN-transformer hybrids, and GNNs.
paperCard.implementation: Implementation steps: 1) Build an emotion graph based on valence-arousal coordinates from Russell's model; 2) During training, add one of three regularization losses: Graph Label Smoothing (simple, distributes probability to adjacent emotions), Graph Laplacian loss (medium, uses pseudo-inverse of Laplacian for global distances), or Sliced Wasserstein loss (complex, uses optimal transport); 3) Balance classification and regularization via fixed weight or adaptive weighting.
paperCard.applications: 1) Mental health monitoring; 2) Affective brain-computer interfaces; 3) Consumer EEG devices (e.g., Muse, Emotiv); 4) Any application needing emotion understanding, such as human-computer interaction, education, and entertainment.
technicalTerms: Graph regularization: Adding a penalty term based on graph structure during training to encourage the model to respect relationships between emotions.
Circumplex model: A psychological theory placing emotions on a 2D circle with valence (positive/negative) and arousal (excited/calm) axes.
Graph Laplacian: A mathematical matrix describing node connections in a graph, used to compute 'commuting distances' between nodes.
Sliced Wasserstein distance: A method to measure difference between two probability distributions by random projections, reducing computational cost.
Proximity Violation: A metric measuring the proportion of misclassifications that cross psychologically distant emotion nodes.
